title: JSON Schema for Atmos Stack Manifest files. Version 1.0. https://atmos.tools

name.description: Logical name for this stack. Takes precedence over name_template and name_pattern from atmos.yaml.

condition_predicate.description: Built-in condition predicate for step and hook execution.

step_condition_predicate.description: Built-in condition predicate for workflow and custom command step execution.

step_condition.description: Declarative condition controlling whether a workflow or custom command step runs. A string is a built-in predicate; an array is shorthand for all predicates; an object composes conditions with all, any, or not.

oneOf.description: All-of shorthand. Every condition in the list must evaluate to true.

all.description: All conditions must evaluate to true.

any.description: At least one condition must evaluate to true.

not.description: The nested condition must evaluate to false.

condition.description: Declarative condition controlling whether a step or hook runs. A string is a built-in predicate; an array is shorthand for all predicates; an object composes conditions with all, any, or not.

oneOf.description: All-of shorthand. Every condition in the list must evaluate to true.

all.description: All conditions must evaluate to true.

any.description: At least one condition must evaluate to true.

not.description: The nested condition must evaluate to false.

ttl.description: Cache duration for the cloned source repo of a remote (git) import. When set, the clone is reused across Atmos invocations until it expires (e.g. with a warm CI cache). Within a single invocation a source repo is cloned at most once regardless of TTL. Examples: '0s', '5m', '1h', '7d', 'daily'.

components.title: components
components.description: Components section. Supports built-in types (terraform, helmfile, kubernetes, packer) and custom component types defined in custom commands.

test.description: Terraform test configuration. `vars` are passed only to `terraform test` as test-scope variables.

container_restart.title: container_restart
container_restart.description: Restart policy (maps to docker/podman --restart). Shared by the container and emulator component kinds.

max_retries.description: Maximum restart attempts (only used with the `on-failure` policy).

container_healthcheck.title: container_healthcheck
container_healthcheck.description: Health check, mirroring the Docker Compose `healthcheck` shape (maps to docker/podman --health-* flags). Shared by the container and emulator component kinds.

test.description: Health command. A string (run via the shell, equivalent to CMD-SHELL) or a list whose first element is NONE, CMD, or CMD-SHELL.

timeout.description: Time a check may take before it is considered failed (e.g. 10s).

retries.description: Consecutive failures before the container is marked unhealthy.

start_period.description: Grace period before failures are counted (e.g. 40s).

start_interval.description: Time between checks during the start period (e.g. 5s).

disable.description: Disable any health check baked into the image.

container_runtime.title: container_runtime
container_runtime.description: Per-instance container runtime block. Shared by the container and emulator component kinds.

provider.description: Container runtime provider: docker, podman, or empty for auto-detect.

auto_start.description: Auto-init/start the Podman machine when no running runtime is found.

host.description: Grant the container access to the host container runtime (Docker-out-of-Docker): mount the runtime socket, run as root, relabel for SELinux, set DOCKER_HOST. Opt-in; effectively host-root. On rootless podman the socket is unreachable in-container — use Docker or `podman machine set --rootful`.

emulator_component_manifest.title: emulator_component_manifest
emulator_component_manifest.description: Emulator component manifest. A driver-backed, long-lived cloud-API emulator (Floci, k3s, OpenBao, registry, …). `driver` and the nested `container` block are first-class sections (not under `vars`).

cloud.description: Explicit emulated cloud/target (optional; derived from the driver when omitted).

services.description: Emulated services to enable (informational; may drive env).

ephemeral.description: When true, run without persisting state (no host data dir is bind-mounted).

container.description: Container overrides for the emulator (image, ports, mounts, env, restart, healthcheck). Reuses the container kind's run shape; the driver supplies defaults.

container_component_manifest.title: container_component_manifest
container_component_manifest.description: Container component manifest. A stack-scoped, persistent container service. `image`, `build`, and `run` are first-class sections (not under `vars`).

composition.description: Name of the composition this component is a member of (must be declared under the top-level `compositions` section).

build.description: Image build configuration (context, dockerfile, tags, build_args, target, etc.).

run.description: Container run configuration (command, ports, mounts, user, restart, healthcheck, etc.).

compositions.title: compositions
compositions.description: Named multi-service systems. Components join a composition via their `composition` field.

services.description: Closed set of service names that may claim membership (open for fulfillment).

kubernetes_paths.title: kubernetes_paths
kubernetes_paths.description: Manifest files or directories relative to the Kubernetes component directory

kubernetes_manifests.title: kubernetes_manifests
kubernetes_manifests.description: Inline Kubernetes manifests rendered and deployed with the component

kubernetes_manifest.title: kubernetes_manifest
kubernetes_manifest.description: Kubernetes API object. Atmos validates only the structural fields it requires before passing the object to the Kubernetes API.

kubernetes_render.title: kubernetes_render
kubernetes_render.description: Render output configuration for Kubernetes manifests

plugins.title: plugins
plugins.description: Helm CLI plugins (e.g. helm-diff, helm-secrets) to install into the managed HELM_PLUGINS directory. Each entry is a compact spec: a short alias (e.g. "diff@3.9.x"), an "owner/repo@version", or a full plugin URL.

generate.title: generate
generate.description: Generate section for declarative file generation.

provision.title: provision
provision.description: Provisioning configuration such as workdir or delivery targets

workdir.description: Working directory provisioning settings for this component

default.description: Name of the provision target used by apply/deploy when --target is not given

targets.description: Named delivery destinations for rendered artifacts, keyed by target name

repository.description: For kind 'git': name of a top-level git.repositories.<name> to publish to

path.description: For kind 'git': destination path inside the deployment repository (supports Go templates)

identity.description: Atmos Auth identity used for the delivery

enabled.description: Publish via pull request (not yet supported by the cli git provider)

dependencies.title: dependencies
dependencies.description: Component, file, or folder dependencies used by affected detection and DAG execution

name.description: Logical component identity used for workspace key prefix auto-generation. Inheritable from base components.

enabled.description: Flag to enable or disable the component

oneOf.description: A list of Atmos components that the current component inherits from
Custom configuration per component, not inherited by derived components
oneOf.title: custom

locked.description: Flag to lock the component and prevent modifications while allowing read operations

key_delimiter.description: Delimiter for expanding dotted keys into nested maps (e.g., '.' or '::'). Empty or omitted disables expansion.

module_paths.description: List of paths to validation modules

locals.title: locals
locals.description: File-scoped local variables for use within templates. Locals are resolved before other sections and can reference each other within the same file. Unlike vars and settings, locals do NOT inherit across file boundaries.

type.description: Step type (step kind): any registered workflow/custom-command step type, e.g. shell, container, http, log, wait, wait-all, cancel. Omitted defaults to a command step.

retry.title: retry
retry.description: Retry configuration for workflow step execution

backoff_strategy.description: Strategy for increasing delay between retries

random_jitter.description: Random jitter factor (0-1) to add to delays

max_elapsed_time.description: Maximum total time for all retry attempts (e.g., '5m', '1h')

working_directory.description: Working directory for the workflow step

identity.description: Identity to use for the workflow step

when.description: Condition controlling whether this workflow step runs. Omitted means run.

interactive.description: Attach host stdin to the step and let the step handle Ctrl-C (like docker -i)

tty.description: Allocate a pseudo-terminal for the step (like docker -t); combine with interactive for full terminal sessions

env.description: Environment variables for the workflow step

tools.description: Require step (type: require/assert): executables that must be found on PATH

files.description: Require step (type: require/assert): paths that must exist

dirs.description: Require step (type: require/assert): directories that must exist

hint.description: Require step (type: require/assert): extra remediation note appended to the failure error

needs.description: Names of earlier steps that must complete before this step runs.

outputs.description: Named outputs exported by the step for use by later steps.

timeout.description: Maximum duration the step may run (e.g. '30s', '5m').

background.description: Run the step asynchronously in the background (boolean; v1 supports this only for container steps). A string value instead sets the step's background style color.

for.description: Fan the step out over a list of values (string expression or explicit array of strings).

action.description: Action selector for action-bearing step kinds (e.g. container: run, build, push, inspect). Selects which params 'with' holds.

provider.description: Provider/runtime backend for the step kind (e.g. container: docker, podman).

container.description: Container sandbox control for the step. Boolean to enable/disable, or an object with sandbox settings.

runtime_auto_start.description: Automatically start the container runtime if it is not already running.

with.description: Parameters for the step named by 'type' (and 'action' where applicable). Validated by the step handler at run time. For container steps this holds image, command, ports, mounts, build context, etc.

backoff_strategy.description: Strategy for increasing delay between retries

random_jitter.description: Random jitter factor (0-1) to add to delays

max_elapsed_time.description: Maximum total time for all retry attempts (e.g., '5m', '1h')

hooks.title: hooks
hooks.description: Hooks section. Each key names a hook; the value sets its kind, the lifecycle events it fires on, and kind-specific settings.

kind.description: Selects the engine that runs this hook.

command.description: Binary to execute (command kind); also the legacy alias for kind (e.g. 'command: store').

events.description: Lifecycle events this hook fires on (e.g. after.terraform.apply). The legacy hyphenated form (e.g. after-terraform-apply) is also accepted. Empty matches all events.

on_failure.description: Failure mode when the hook errors.

when.description: Condition controlling whether this hook runs. Omitted means success. If no success, failure, or always predicate is mentioned, success is applied implicitly.

type.description: Step type to run (step kind): any registered workflow/custom-command step type, e.g. container, http, toast, log.

with.description: Parameters for the step named by 'type' (step kind), or an ordered list of steps (steps kind). Validated by the step handler at run time.

component_secrets.title: component_secrets
component_secrets.description: Secrets configuration. Declares secrets resolved at runtime via the `!secret` YAML function, and (at the stack/global level) the non-store secret providers (e.g. SOPS) that back them. Store-backed secrets reference a `secret: true` store under `stores`.

secret_providers.title: secret_providers
secret_providers.description: Non-store secret providers (e.g. SOPS) that do not fit the key-value store interface. Keys are provider names, values are provider configurations.

secret_provider.title: secret_provider
secret_provider.description: A single non-store secret provider configuration (e.g. a SOPS backend).

identity.description: Optional auth identity (resolved via auth) used by KMS-backed SOPS kinds (sops/aws-kms, sops/gcp-kms). Local-key kinds (sops/age, sops/gpg) ignore it.

spec.description: Provider-specific options (e.g. file, age_recipients, kms arn).

secret_vars.title: secret_vars
secret_vars.description: Declared secrets for a component. Keys are secret names, values are secret declarations. A secret must be declared before it can be set, read via `!secret`, or pulled/pushed.

secret_declaration.title: secret_declaration
secret_declaration.description: A single declared secret. Exactly one backend reference (store or sops) is set.

store.description: Names a `secret: true` store (under `stores`) this secret resolves from.

sops.description: Names a `secrets.providers` SOPS provider this secret resolves from.

reference.description: Optional backend-specific address for the secret. Reference-based backends (e.g. a 1Password store) resolve it directly instead of composing a key from the secret name; it may contain Go-template vars ({{ .atmos_stack }}, {{ .atmos_component }}). Example: "op://Production/{{ .atmos_component }}/password". Name-keyed backends ignore it.

required.description: Marks the secret as required; validation fails if it is not initialized.

scope.description: Storage scope. Normally derived from declaration position (top-level `secrets:` => stack, shared by every component instance; `components.<c>.secrets:` => instance). If set explicitly it must match the position — a conflict (e.g. `scope: stack` under a component) is an error — except `global`, which is honored at either position. Instance-scoped secrets are stored per component; stack-scoped secrets are stored once per stack; global secrets are stored once per backend store and shared by every stack and component that resolves through it.

component_auth.title: component_auth
component_auth.description: Component-level authentication configuration. Allows overriding or extending global auth providers and identities for specific components.

realm.description: Credential isolation realm. Different realms provide isolated credentials even for identities with the same name.

auth_providers.title: auth_providers
auth_providers.description: Authentication providers configuration. Keys are provider names, values are provider configurations.

start_url.description: SSO start URL for AWS IAM Identity Center

default.description: Whether this is the default provider

auto_provision_identities.description: Whether to auto-provision identities when authenticating with this provider

auth_identities.title: auth_identities
auth_identities.description: Authentication identities configuration. Keys are identity names, values are identity configurations.

default.description: Whether this is the default identity

required.description: Whether this identity is automatically authenticated without prompting. Required identities are authenticated before commands run.

provider.description: Provider name for direct provider association

name.description: Principal name (e.g., permission set name, role name)

env.description: Environment variables to set when using this identity

auth_identity_via.title: auth_identity_via
auth_identity_via.description: Specifies how an identity connects to a provider or chains from another identity.

identity.description: Identity name to chain from (for role chaining)

session_duration.description: Console session duration (e.g., '12h'). Max: 12h for AWS.
